summary: FDNY's Bureau of Fire Prevention has steadily grown and trained its civilian Fire Protection Inspector workforce, graduating new classes of inspectors and expanding capacity year over year; completed inspections rose from about 149,000 in FY22 to more than 205,000 in FY25, comfortably clearing the City's own target. The Bureau conducted nine percent more inspections in the first four months of FY26 alone compared to the same period a year earlier, alongside increases in violations issued and corrected, as the expanded inspector corps keeps pace with a larger caseload.

sources.label: FDNY, Preliminary Fiscal 2026 Mayor's Management Report
JoinFDNY, "From the Fire Prevention Academy to the Bureau of Fire Prevention"

oneLiner: increasing as FDNY has steadily grown and trained its civilian Fire Prevention Inspector workforce
